title: Do not default to illustrated empty states
summary: AI-generated empty states consistently use a centered line-art illustration above a 'Nothing here yet' headline, subdued subtext, and a 'Get started' CTA — treating emptiness as decoration opportunity rather than guidance moment.
instruction: Treat empty states as guidance opportunities, not decoration slots. Replace generic illustrations with precise next-step instructions, sample data previews, or contextual onboarding that helps the user populate the space.
rationale: The illustration-first empty state prioritizes decoration over direction. Users encountering an empty state need to understand what happened and what to do next — a cute illustration and generic message delay that understanding.
violations: Centered line-art or geometric illustration as the primary visual
'Nothing here yet', 'No data', or 'This page is empty' headline
Subdued subtext that describes the emptiness rather than the next action
Primary filled 'Get started' or 'Add your first X' CTA button
Same empty state format regardless of brand tone or user context
controls: Task-oriented empty state with numbered next steps
Sample/preview data shown instead of blank space
Contextual guidance that explains why the state is empty and how to change it
Empty state that integrates with onboarding flow
borderlineCases: Consumer apps where playful empty states match the brand personality
contextualExceptions: Splash-style first-run experiences where illustration sets the tone
detectorSignal: A centered container with an illustration/graphic, a heading-level text indicating absence, a paragraph of explanatory text, and a primary call-to-action button.
matcherProposal: Detect a centered layout containing an image/illustration, a heading with emptiness keywords ('no data', 'nothing here', 'empty', 'no items'), and a primary button.
detectorLimitations: Cannot determine if the illustration serves a genuine brand purpose
falsePositiveScenarios: Branded illustration style that is part of a deliberate design system
mitigationStrategy: Flag as a suggestion to evaluate whether the illustration helps the user or just fills space.

fixturePlans.violation: Centered empty state with line-art illustration, 'Nothing here yet' headline, 'Start by adding your first item' subtext, 'Get Started' button.
fixturePlans.control: Empty state with sample data preview, numbered steps, 'Here's what to do first' guidance.
fixturePlans.borderline: Consumer app with brand-mascot illustration as part of personality.

cleanRoomAttestations: Developed without access to proprietary prompt collections.
